---
title: "Module 01: Foundations and Generalization"
---

## Learning objectives

- Frame **supervised learning** as predicting an outcome from measured inputs, with a clear train / validation / test story.
- Connect **bias–variance** and “too wiggly vs too stiff” to plots students already see on Monday.
- Start the habit of a **data card**: what was measured, who is missing, what you would not claim from a score alone.

## Key messages

- **Simple baselines** (linear models, clear splits) anchor everything that comes later.
- The course uses **two data tracks**: synthetic **genes** (known ground truth) and **[Palmer Penguins](../dataset-penguins.qmd)** (one real table all week).

## Concept outline

### Supervised setup

- Rows are units (birds, patients, assays); columns are outcomes and predictors.
- **Generalization** is the goal: performance on **new** rows from the same kind of process, not memorizing the training table.

### Splits and honest evaluation

- **Training** fits parameters; **validation** (or cross-validation) chooses complexity or hyperparameters; **test** (or a locked holdout) is touched rarely.
- Say explicitly why **preprocessing that learns from data** (means, factor levels, PCA) must be **inside** the resampling fold or workflow, not “fit on all rows first.”

### Bias and variance (Monday vocabulary)

- **Underfitting:** train and validation both look bad — model too simple or features too weak.
- **Overfitting:** train looks great, validation bad — model too flexible or too many effective degrees of freedom for *n*.

### Data card (lightweight)

- Provenance, inclusion criteria, missingness, sensitive or grouping fields (species, island as subgroup lenses on penguins).
- One paragraph students can reuse in homework.

## Course links

- **Slides:** [Monday — Foundations and regularization](../slides/day-01-monday.qmd)
- **Curriculum:** [Curriculum outline](../curriculum-outline.qmd) (Monday: Foundations and Regularization)
- **Notebooks — genes:** [linear + LASSO](../notebooks/linear-regression-lasso.Rmd), [logistic + tree](../notebooks/logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd)
- **Notebooks — penguins:** [body mass](../notebooks/penguins-body-mass.Rmd), [classification](../notebooks/penguins-classification.Rmd), [sex](../notebooks/penguins-sex-classification.Rmd), [three species](../notebooks/penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd)
- **Data reference:** [Palmer Penguins](../dataset-penguins.qmd)
- **Module library:** [Back to all modules](index.qmd)

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## Draft / TODO

- Add one **worked numeric example** of train vs validation loss (optional small table or figure) without duplicating the full Monday slide include.
- Link or embed a **one-page data card template** when the course settles on a PDF/DOCX version.
:::
